headline: The Curd Fryers Are Serving Wisconsin's Cheesiest Beatdown and I'm Here For It
subheadline: Week 21 gave us a 9-1 massacre, the Skidmarks continuing their improbable rise, and standings that look like my fantasy baseball commissioner fell asleep at the keyboard.
body:
Before we dive in, can we talk about these standings? Every team at 0-0 with zero points for? This is either a massive database glitch or we've entered some kind of baseball purgatory where nothing matters and the points are made up (Drew Carey voice). I'm choosing to believe it's Week 21 of a points-only league and someone just forgot to carry the one. Moving on.

**Central Wisconsin Curd Fryers** absolutely demolished the **Canberra Wombats** 9.0-1.0, and honestly, this felt like watching the Mountain fight Oberyn Martell. You knew it was going to be ugly, but you couldn't look away. The Wombats showed up expecting a baseball game and instead got deep-fried in Wisconsin's finest canola oil. (Side note: I'm convinced the Curd Fryers have the best team name in this league, and it's not particularly close. You're telling me someone sat down, thought about cheese curds at a county fair, and said 'Yeah, that's my identity now'? Respect.)

The **Buttville Skidmarks** (I can't believe I'm typing this professionally) squeaked past **Hiroshima Radiation** 5.0-4.0 in what has to be the most unfortunately-named matchup in fantasy sports history. But here's the thing—the Skidmarks keep winning ugly, which is basically the 2004 Red Sox playbook minus the flowing locks and Pedro's Dominican fire. A win's a win, even if your team name makes your mom pretend she doesn't know you at Thanksgiving.

**General Hospital** handled **North Correa** 7.0-3.0, and yes, I'm legally obligated to mention that General Hospital is both a functioning fantasy team AND has been on the air since 1963. That show has outlasted seven presidencies, three different Spider-Men, and apparently North Correa's playoff hopes.

The **Abbotsford-Colby Twins** and **Riverside Roughnecks** tied 5.0-5.0, which in fantasy baseball is the equivalent of kissing your sister (if your sister was also somehow your cousin from the weird side of the family). Nobody wanted this outcome. Nobody.

Looking ahead: with those standings looking like a freshly reset Excel spreadsheet, every team's technically in the hunt. That's either beautiful parity or complete chaos. Probably both.

(The **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** won too, and their name is clearly a Bob's Burgers reference, which automatically makes them my second-favorite team behind the Curd Fryers. I don't make the rules.)
